scenario: An insurer models a Camunda 8.8 invoice process that handles 3500 invoices per day. A modeler wants to know which BPMN start event types are supported for execution in Camunda 8 and which are documented as not yet runnable so the team can avoid drawing a 5-page diagram that will not start in production.
question: Which BPMN start event from the routing-events table is listed as not yet supported for execution in Camunda 8?

options.text: The conditional start event is documented as having no execution support; the page tells you to determine the occurrence of the condition externally yourself and use a message event to start the process
The signal start event is documented as having no execution support; the page tells you to determine the occurrence of the signal externally and use a message event to start the process
The timer start event is documented as having no execution support; the page tells you to determine the schedule externally and use a message event to start the process
The none start event is documented as having no execution support; the page tells you to determine the standard start externally and use a message event to start the process

a.text: Correct. In the start-events table, the conditional event's Supported for Execution cell does not show a check mark and instead instructs you to determine occurrence of the condition externally yourself and use the message event, while none, message, timer, and signal start events are all marked supported.

b.text: Incorrect. The signal start event is shown as supported for execution in the table; the documented use case is to start several process instances at once.

c.text: Incorrect. The timer start event is shown as supported for execution and is documented for time-controlled automatic starts of process instances.

d.text: Incorrect. The none start event is shown as supported for execution and is described as the standard or single starting point of a process definition.

explanation: The start-events table on the routing-events-to-processes page marks none, message, timer, and signal start events as supported for execution. Only the conditional start event is not directly supported; the recommendation is to externally detect the condition and use a message event instead.
